req.title: KYC refresh (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days; accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement has four testable facets: (1) successful KYC refresh within window sets verified=true (EP positive path); (2) idempotency — duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key returns original response without side-effects (STC, since the system transitions from 'submitted' back to same state); (3) boundary — submission exactly on day 365 (last valid day, BVA upper boundary); (4) account beyond 365-day window restricted to inbound only (STC state restriction); (5) compliance failure returns 403 (NEG). Five tests cover all mandatory kinds (positive, negative, boundary) plus extra STC coverage for the restriction state and idempotency.

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_valid_customer_sets_verified
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last KYC refresh was fewer than 365 days ago
Compliance check will pass for the submitted data
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with valid KYC payload and unique Idempotency-Key header
Inspect HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a refreshed timestamp within current day
trace: R-FIN-090: customer.verified=true refreshed on successful submission
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date

def test_kyc_refresh_valid_customer_sets_verified(api_client, valid_kyc_payload, auth_headers):
    idempotency_key = <generate_idempotency_key>()
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=valid_kyc_payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["customer"]["verified"] is True
    assert "refreshed_at" in body["customer"]
    refreshed_date = body["customer"]["refreshed_at"][:10]
    assert refreshed_date == str(date.today())

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_result
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
A KYC refresh has already been submitted successfully with a specific Idempotency-Key
The same Idempotency-Key is reused for the duplicate request
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with valid KYC payload and Idempotency-Key X (first submission)
Record the response body and status code
POST /kyc/refresh again with identical payload and same Idempotency-Key X
expected: Second request returns same HTTP status code as first
Second response body is identical to first response body
No duplicate KYC record is created (state unchanged after second call)
trace: R-FIN-090: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return the original result
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_duplicate_idempotency_key_returns_original_result(api_client, valid_kyc_payload, auth_headers):
    idempotency_key = <generate_idempotency_key>()
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    first = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=valid_kyc_payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code in (200, 201)

    second = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=valid_kyc_payload, headers=headers)

    assert second.status_code == first.status_code
    assert second.json() == first.json(), "Idempotent responses must be identical"

    audit = api_client.get("/kyc/refresh/audit", headers=auth_headers, params={"key": idempotency_key})
    assert audit.json()["submission_count"] == 1, "Duplicate must not create a second record"

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_exactly_on_day_365_is_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last KYC refresh timestamp is set to exactly 365 days ago (boundary day)
actions: Seed customer record so last_kyc_refresh = today - 365 days via <seed_kyc_date> fixture
POST /kyc/refresh with valid KYC payload and unique Idempotency-Key
Inspect HTTP response status and verified flag
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 status code (day 365 is still within the valid window)
trace: R-FIN-090: accounts beyond refresh window restricted; day 365 is the upper inclusive boundary
BVA: test at exact boundary value of 365 days
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_kyc_refresh_exactly_on_day_365_is_accepted(api_client, auth_headers, seed_kyc_date):
    boundary_date = date.today() - timedelta(days=365)
    seed_kyc_date(boundary_date)

    idempotency_key = <generate_idempotency_key>()
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}
    payload = <valid_kyc_payload>()

    resp = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Day-365 boundary should be accepted, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    assert resp.json()["customer"]["verified"] is True

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_account_beyond_365_days_restricted_to_inbound_only
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
Customer last KYC refresh timestamp is set to 366 days ago (one day past the boundary)
actions: Seed customer record so last_kyc_refresh = today - 366 days via <seed_kyc_date> fixture
Attempt an outbound transaction via POST /transactions (outbound)
Attempt an inbound transaction via POST /transactions (inbound)
expected: Outbound transaction returns HTTP 403 or 423 (account restricted)
Inbound transaction returns HTTP 200 or 201 (inbound still permitted)
trace: R-FIN-090: accounts beyond refresh window restricted to inbound transactions only
STC: transition from 'active' state to 'restricted' state at day 366
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_kyc_refresh_account_beyond_365_days_restricted_to_inbound_only(
    api_client, auth_headers, seed_kyc_date
):
    expired_date = date.today() - timedelta(days=366)
    seed_kyc_date(expired_date)

    outbound_payload = <build_transaction_payload>(direction="outbound", amount=100)
    out_resp = api_client.post("/transactions", json=outbound_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert out_resp.status_code in (403, 423), (
        f"Outbound must be blocked beyond window, got {out_resp.status_code}"
    )

    inbound_payload = <build_transaction_payload>(direction="inbound", amount=100)
    in_resp = api_client.post("/transactions", json=inbound_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert in_resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Inbound must be allowed beyond window, got {in_resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_compliance_failure_returns_403
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid JWT
KYC payload contains data that will fail compliance checks (e.g. blacklisted entity)
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with non-compliant KYC payload and unique Idempotency-Key
Inspect HTTP response status and error body
expected: Response body contains an error code or message indicating compliance failure
trace: NEG: error pathway for compliance rejection
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_kyc_refresh_compliance_failure_returns_403(api_client, auth_headers, non_compliant_kyc_payload):
    idempotency_key = <generate_idempotency_key>()
    headers = {**auth_headers, "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post("/kyc/refresh", json=non_compliant_kyc_payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403 for compliance failure, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "Response must contain error detail"
    error_text = body.get("error", body.get("message", "")).lower()
    assert "compliance" in error_text or "rejected" in error_text or "forbidden" in error_text

    profile_resp = api_client.get("/customers/me", headers=auth_headers)
    assert profile_resp.json()["customer"]["verified"] is not True
